title: Runtime Configuration Studio
concept: A settings-first, learner-oriented WordPress Playground workspace that makes WordPress, PHP, language, network, multisite, reset, and reload decisions the first visible task before routing users into launch, saved management, Site Manager tools, blueprint gallery, and portability flows.
focus: high-contrast black white and cyan technical palette
flows: Configure WordPress version, older versions, PHP version, language, network access, and multisite before starting or resetting a Playground.
Start a new Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, zip import, featured blueprints, or the full gallery.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress, then manage saved and unsaved sites with rename and delete actions.
Use Site Manager tabs for settings, file browser, Blueprint editing, database tools, logs, Homepage, WP Admin, export to GitHub, and zip download.
Browse and filter all Blueprint gallery entries before running a selected Blueprint.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
understand destructive reset and saved reload behavior
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
